title: gtp-25-1462
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Okay, here's the extracted technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ΔPmid = 0.3% (Pressure difference between medium temperature section and the main air section.)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*Table 1: Optically Accessible Combustion Rig*

|                             |           |          |       |
| :-------------------------- | :-------- | :------- | :---- |
| Air flow                    | Main air  | 14.5 kg/s | 32 lb/s |
|                             | Cooling air | 6.6 kg/s |14 lb/s |
| Air temperature             | Main air  | 922 K    | 1200 F |
|                             | Cooling air | 373 K    | 212 F  |
| Pressure absolute           |          | 2068 kPa / 20.68 bar | 300 psi|
| Hydrogen flow               |          | 0.10 kg/s | 0.22 lb/s|
| Natural gas flow            |          | 0.68 kg/s  | 1.5 lb/s|
| Test section               |Diameter      | 45 cm|18 in|
|            |Length      | 120cm      | 47 in|
| Optical access              | Midsection| 4 (0°, 90°, 135°, 270°) | |
|                             | Exhaust   | 1          |        |

**4. Diagrams:**

**Figure 1(a) and 1(b):** (Description from text): Shows the OACR geometry. Figure 1(a) highlights the air inlet section, cooling air section, and exhaust section. Figure 1(b) shows how the fuel is delivered to the combustion system. Emissions are measured in the exhaust section.
The fuel inlet flange has four ports to accommodate primary (Py) and secondary (Sy) fuel streams for both natural gas and hydrogen. Two ports are used for each fuel stage to ensure a uniform fuel feed to the internal fuel manifold. The different fuel components are teed-in and mixed inside the pressure vessel before the fuel blend is delivered to the combustion system

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Air flow (Main air): 14.5 kg/s, 32 lb/s
*   Air flow (Cooling air): 6.6 kg/s, 14 lb/s
*   Air temperature (Main air): 922 K, 1200 F
*   Air temperature (Cooling air): 373 K, 212 F
*   Pressure absolute: 2068 kPa, 20.68 bar, 300 psi
*   Hydrogen flow: 0.10 kg/s, 0.22 lb/s
*   Natural gas flow: 0.68 kg/s, 1.5 lb/s
*   Test section diameter: 45 cm (18 in)
*   Test section length: 120 cm (47 in)
*   Optical access: 4 ports at 0°, 90°, 135°, 270° (midsection), 1 (exhaust).
*   Maximum preheated air temperature: 922 K
*   Low-temperature air (cooling): up to 373 K
*   Internal diameter of cooling section: 0.45 m
*

title: gtp-25-1462
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**

    *   MFR = Vsy / (Vpy + Vsy)

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**
     NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  **Tables:**
     NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:** Description of components from Figure 1.
    *   **(a) External pressure vessel:**
        *   Exhaust, water-jacket cooling
        *   Medium temperature
        *   Fuel inlet flange
        *   Air inlet
        *   Hot exhaust
        *   Emissions probe
        *   Optical access (0°, 90°, 270°)
        *   Igniter
    *   **(b) Combustion rig housed in the medium temperature section:**
        *   283nm laser
        *   Sy
        *   Cooling
        *   Air
        *   NG
        *   H2
        *   Py
        *   Igniter
        *   Emissions probe
        *   PTComb,0-1
        *   Exhaust tube
        *   Heat shield
        *   PT Air, 90-1
        *   PT Air, 90-2
        *   PTComb, 180-1
        *   PTComb, 180-2
    *   **(c) Cross section of the combustion system at the location of the laser sheet used for diagnostics:**
        *   283nm laser
        *   Sy
        *   Cooling
        *   Py
        *   PTComb, 180-1
        *   PTComb, 180-2

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

    *   MFR100 corresponding to 100% micromixed combustion
    *   Inconel 625 (material)
    *   EOS M290 machine
    *   7 injectors
    *   Type-N thermocouples (temperature measurement)
    *   5 dynamic pressure transducers CP211
    *   PTAir,90-1
    *   PTAir,90-2
    *   PTComb,0-1
    *   PTComb,180-1
    *   PTComb,180-2
    *   50 kHz sampling frequency
    *   Laser path 8.5 m long
    *   80 psig (purge pressure)
    *   Spectra-Physics Nd:YAG laser (Quanta-Ray Pro-250-10H)
    *   Rhodamine 6G (dye solution)
    *   282.94 nm (dye laser wavelength)
    *   ~8.5 mJ/pulse (laser output energy)

title: gtp-25-1462
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Piecewise, exponential fit performed to approximate the Beer-Lambert laser attenuation.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 7(a): Moving average - Row (x)**
    *   Axes: x/D [-], z/D [-]
    *   Curves: Colormap showing the moving average of intensities
    *   Key values: x/D from -2 to 2, z/D from 0 to 3
*   **Fig. 7(b): Gaussian smoothing - Column (z)**
    *   Axes: x/D [-], z/D [-]
    *   Curves: Colormap showing the Gaussian smoothed intensities
    *   Key values: x/D from -2 to 2, z/D from 0 to 3
*   **Fig. 7(c): Local intensity fluctuation**
    *   Axes: x/D [-], z/D [-]
    *   Curves: Regions of local intensity fluctuation
    *   Key values: x/D from -2 to 2, z/D from 0 to 3
*   **Fig. 8: Projection of OH intensity**
    *   Axes: x/D [-], OH (a.u.)
    *   Curves: Scatter plot of OH intensity vs. x/D, Piecewise exponential fit
    *   Key values: x/D from -2 to 2, OH (a.u.) from 0 to 4000

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   12% ROI smoothing window
*   0.4D moving window (Gaussian Smoothing)
*   OH intensity projection between 0.7-1.4z/D
*   10 bar condition
*   5, 10, and 15 bar (pressures)
*   SNR between 1.3 and 2 (Signal to Noise Ratio)
* Injector trios I100, I180, I240 and I100, I160, I120
* The pattern is repeated around the central injector spaced by 120 deg between the injector trios.
